testCases.title: The user should be able to complete registration when valid email and compliant password are provided
preconditions: App is installed and launched on a mobile device
User is on the Registration screen
The email address used is not already registered in the staging database
Password complexity rules are documented and known
Test email service (e.g., Mailtrap) is configured and accessible

steps.content: Navigate to the Registration screen
steps.expected: Registration screen is displayed with email and password input fields

steps.content: Enter a valid, unregistered email address in the email field (e.g., newuser@example.com)
steps.expected: Email is accepted and displayed in the field without inline errors

steps.content: Enter a password that meets all complexity requirements in the password field
steps.expected: Password is masked by default and accepted without inline errors

steps.content: Tap the Register / Submit button
steps.expected: A loading indicator is shown and the form submission is initiated

steps.content: Wait for the system response
steps.expected: User is successfully registered; a confirmation screen or success message is displayed, and/or a confirmation email is sent to the registered address

testCases.expectedResult: The user account is created successfully. The user is navigated to the appropriate post-registration screen (e.g., dashboard or email verification prompt). No duplicate account is created.

testCases.title: The registration form should display an inline error when the submitted email address is already registered
preconditions: App is installed and launched on a mobile device
User is on the Registration screen
A valid user account with the target email already exists in the staging database

steps.content: Navigate to the Registration screen
steps.expected: Registration screen is displayed

steps.content: Enter an email address that is already registered in the system
steps.expected: Email is entered in the field

steps.content: Enter a valid, compliant password in the password field
steps.expected: Password is entered and masked

steps.content: Tap the Register / Submit button
steps.expected: Form submission is initiated

steps.content: Wait for the system response
steps.expected: An inline error message is displayed indicating the email address is already in use. No new account is created.

testCases.expectedResult: The system rejects the registration attempt and displays a clear, inline error message stating the email is already associated with an account. The user is not logged in automatically.

testCases.title: The registration form should display a validation error when all fields are submitted empty
preconditions: App is installed and launched on a mobile device
User is on the Registration screen
No data has been entered in any field

steps.content: Navigate to the Registration screen
steps.expected: Registration screen is displayed with empty email and password fields

steps.content: Tap the Register / Submit button without entering any data
steps.expected: Form submission is attempted

steps.content: Observe the form state
steps.expected: Validation errors are displayed on all required fields (email and password). The form is not submitted to the backend.

testCases.expectedResult: The system prevents form submission and displays inline validation errors on all empty required fields. No API call is made.

testCases.title: The password field should mask input by default and reveal it when the user toggles visibility during registration
preconditions: App is installed and launched on a mobile device
User is on the Registration screen

steps.content: Navigate to the Registration screen
steps.expected: Registration screen is displayed

steps.content: Tap on the password input field and type any characters
steps.expected: Characters are entered but displayed as masked dots/asterisks by default

steps.content: Tap the password visibility toggle icon (show/hide)
steps.expected: The password text is revealed in plain text

steps.content: Tap the password visibility toggle icon again
steps.expected: The password text is masked again

testCases.expectedResult: The password field masks input by default. The visibility toggle correctly switches between masked and plain-text states on each tap.

testCases.title: The system should not create a duplicate account when the same registration form is submitted twice in rapid succession
preconditions: App is installed and launched on a mobile device
User is on the Registration screen
The email address used is not already registered

steps.content: Navigate to the Registration screen
steps.expected: Registration screen is displayed

steps.content: Enter a valid, unregistered email and a compliant password
steps.expected: Fields are populated correctly

steps.content: Tap the Register / Submit button twice in rapid succession (double-tap)
steps.expected: The button is disabled or a loading state is shown after the first tap to prevent duplicate submissions

steps.content: Wait for the system response
steps.expected: Only one account is created. No duplicate registration error or duplicate record appears in the database.

testCases.expectedResult: Exactly one user account is created. The system handles the double-tap gracefully, either by disabling the button after the first tap or by backend idempotency checks.

testCases.title: The registration form should preserve entered data when the user navigates away and returns via back navigation
preconditions: App is installed and launched on a mobile device
User is on the Registration screen
User has partially filled in the registration form

steps.content: Navigate to the Registration screen
steps.expected: Registration screen is displayed

steps.content: Enter a valid email address in the email field
steps.expected: Email is displayed in the field

steps.content: Enter a partial or complete password in the password field
steps.expected: Password is masked in the field

steps.content: Tap the device back button or navigate to a previous screen
steps.expected: User is navigated away from the Registration screen

steps.content: Navigate back to the Registration screen
steps.expected: Observe whether previously entered data is preserved or cleared

testCases.expectedResult: The behavior is noted and compared against product expectations. If the PRD or design specifies data preservation, fields should retain their values. If not specified, the result is flagged for PO clarification.

testCases.title: The user should be able to log in successfully when valid registered credentials are entered
preconditions: App is installed and launched on a mobile device
User is on the Login screen
A valid user account (email + password) is pre-seeded in the staging database

steps.content: Navigate to the Login screen
steps.expected: Login screen is displayed with email and password input fields

steps.content: Enter the registered email address in the email field
steps.expected: Email is accepted and displayed without errors

steps.content: Enter the correct password in the password field
steps.expected: Password is masked and accepted without errors

steps.content: Tap the Login / Sign In button
steps.expected: A loading indicator is shown and the login request is initiated

steps.content: Wait for the system response and measure elapsed time
steps.expected: User is authenticated and navigated to the dashboard or home screen within 3 seconds

testCases.expectedResult: The user is successfully logged in and redirected to the post-login screen. The total login response time is under 3 seconds. A valid session token is issued and securely stored.

testCases.title: The login form should display a generic error message when invalid credentials are submitted
preconditions: App is installed and launched on a mobile device
User is on the Login screen

steps.content: Navigate to the Login screen
steps.expected: Login screen is displayed

steps.content: Enter a valid email format that is registered in the system
steps.expected: Email is entered in the field

steps.content: Enter an incorrect password
steps.expected: Password is entered and masked

steps.content: Tap the Login / Sign In button
steps.expected: Login request is submitted

steps.content: Wait for the system response
steps.expected: A generic error message is displayed (e.g., 'Invalid email or password') without specifying which field is incorrect, to prevent user enumeration

testCases.expectedResult: The login attempt fails. A generic, non-specific error message is shown. The user is not logged in. The error message does not reveal whether the email or password is the incorrect field.

testCases.title: The login form should display a meaningful error when the server is unreachable during a login attempt
preconditions: App is installed and launched on a mobile device
User is on the Login screen
Device network connectivity is disabled or the server is simulated as unreachable (e.g., via Charles Proxy)

steps.content: Disable network connectivity on the device or simulate server unavailability
steps.expected: Device has no active network connection or server is unreachable

steps.content: Enter a valid email and password on the Login screen
steps.expected: Credentials are entered in the fields

steps.content: Tap the Login / Sign In button
steps.expected: Login request is initiated

steps.content: Wait for the system response or timeout
steps.expected: A meaningful error message is displayed (e.g., 'No internet connection. Please try again.') and the loading state is dismissed

testCases.expectedResult: The app handles the network failure gracefully. A user-friendly error message is displayed. The app does not crash or hang indefinitely.

testCases.title: The SSO flow should not proceed when network connectivity is lost during the OAuth handshake
preconditions: App is installed and launched on a mobile device
User is on the Login screen
A Google test account is available

steps.content: Navigate to the Login screen
steps.expected: Login screen is displayed

steps.content: Tap the 'Sign in with Google' button
steps.expected: The native Google account picker is displayed

steps.content: Select the Google test account
steps.expected: OAuth handshake begins

steps.content: Disable network connectivity on the device immediately after account selection
steps.expected: Network is disconnected during the OAuth flow

steps.content: Wait for the system response or timeout
steps.expected: A meaningful error message is displayed (e.g., 'Unable to connect. Please check your internet connection.'). The app does not crash or hang.

testCases.expectedResult: The SSO flow fails gracefully when network is lost mid-handshake. A user-friendly error is displayed. No partial session is created. The user can retry.

testCases.title: The system behavior should be verified when a Google SSO email matches an existing email/password account
preconditions: App is installed and launched on a mobile device
User is on the Login screen
An email/password account exists in the staging database with the same email as the Google test account
Expected behavior (merge vs. error) has been confirmed with the Product Owner

steps.content: Navigate to the Login screen
steps.expected: Login screen is displayed

steps.content: Tap the 'Sign in with Google' button
steps.expected: The native Google account picker is displayed

steps.content: Select the Google test account whose email matches an existing email/password account
steps.expected: OAuth handshake is initiated

steps.content: Wait for the system response
steps.expected: System responds according to the confirmed PO behavior: either accounts are merged and the user is logged in, or an error/prompt is displayed indicating the email is already registered via a different method

testCases.expectedResult: The system handles the account conflict according to the PO-confirmed behavior (merge or error). No data loss or silent account duplication occurs.

testCases.title: The system should send a password reset email when a valid registered email is submitted in the forgot password form
preconditions: App is installed and launched on a mobile device
User is on the Login screen
A valid registered email account exists in the staging database
Test email service (e.g., Mailtrap) is configured and accessible to the QA team
Device has active network connectivity

steps.content: Navigate to the Login screen
steps.expected: Login screen is displayed

steps.content: Tap the 'Forgot Password' link or button
steps.expected: Forgot Password screen is displayed with an email input field

steps.content: Enter the valid registered email address in the email field
steps.expected: Email is accepted and displayed in the field

steps.content: Tap the Submit / Send Reset Link button
steps.expected: A loading indicator is shown and the request is submitted

steps.content: Wait for the system response
steps.expected: A confirmation message is displayed on screen (e.g., 'If this email is registered, you will receive a reset link shortly')

steps.content: Check the test email inbox for the reset email
steps.expected: A password reset email is received containing a valid reset link

testCases.expectedResult: A password reset email is delivered to the registered email address. The on-screen confirmation message is displayed. The reset link in the email is functional.

testCases.title: The forgot password form should display a neutral confirmation message when an unregistered email is submitted
preconditions: App is installed and launched on a mobile device
User is on the Forgot Password screen
The email address to be entered is confirmed as not registered in the staging database

steps.content: Navigate to the Forgot Password screen
steps.expected: Forgot Password screen is displayed

steps.content: Enter an email address that is not registered in the system
steps.expected: Email is entered in the field

steps.content: Tap the Submit / Send Reset Link button
steps.expected: Request is submitted

steps.content: Wait for the system response
steps.expected: The same neutral confirmation message is displayed as for a registered email (e.g., 'If this email is registered, you will receive a reset link shortly'). No error indicating the email does not exist is shown.

steps.content: Check the test email inbox for any email sent to the unregistered address
steps.expected: No password reset email is received for the unregistered address

testCases.expectedResult: The system displays a neutral, non-revealing confirmation message regardless of whether the email is registered. No reset email is sent to the unregistered address. User enumeration is prevented.

testCases.title: The password reset link should be invalid after it has been used once
preconditions: A valid password reset email has been received in the test inbox
The reset link has not been used yet
Device has active network connectivity

steps.content: Open the password reset link from the test email inbox for the first time
steps.expected: The reset password screen is displayed, allowing the user to set a new password

steps.content: Enter a new valid, compliant password and confirm it
steps.expected: New password is entered and accepted

steps.content: Tap the Submit / Reset Password button
steps.expected: Password is reset successfully and a success confirmation is shown

steps.content: Open the same password reset link again (reuse attempt)
steps.expected: An error message is displayed indicating the link is invalid, expired, or already used. The reset password form is not shown.

testCases.expectedResult: The password reset link is single-use. After the first successful use, any subsequent attempt to use the same link results in an error. The link cannot be replayed.

testCases.title: The password reset link should be invalid when accessed after the expiry time limit has passed
preconditions: A valid password reset email has been received in the test inbox
The reset link expiry window is known and confirmed with the backend team
A mechanism exists to simulate or fast-forward link expiry in the QA environment (e.g., expired token pre-generated by backend)

steps.content: Obtain an expired password reset link (either by waiting past the expiry window or using a pre-generated expired token from the backend)
steps.expected: An expired reset link is available

steps.content: Open the expired password reset link
steps.expected: The app or browser processes the link

steps.content: Observe the system response
steps.expected: An error message is displayed indicating the link has expired (e.g., 'This reset link has expired. Please request a new one.'). The reset password form is not accessible.

testCases.expectedResult: The expired reset link is rejected by the system. A clear expiry error message is shown. The user is prompted or directed to request a new reset link.

testCases.title: The reset password form should enforce password complexity rules when the user sets a new password
preconditions: User has opened a valid, unused password reset link
The reset password screen is displayed
Password complexity rules are documented and known

steps.content: On the reset password screen, enter a password that does not meet complexity requirements (e.g., too short, missing special characters)
steps.expected: Password is entered in the field

steps.content: Tap the Submit / Reset Password button
steps.expected: Form submission is attempted

steps.content: Observe the system response
steps.expected: An inline validation error is displayed describing the unmet complexity requirements. The password is not reset.

testCases.expectedResult: The system enforces the same password complexity rules on the reset flow as on registration. A non-compliant password is rejected with a descriptive inline error.

testCases.title: The system should handle gracefully when multiple password reset emails are requested in rapid succession for the same email
preconditions: App is installed and launched on a mobile device
User is on the Forgot Password screen
A valid registered email is available
Device has active network connectivity

steps.content: Navigate to the Forgot Password screen
steps.expected: Forgot Password screen is displayed

steps.content: Enter a valid registered email address
steps.expected: Email is entered in the field

steps.content: Tap the Submit button
steps.expected: First reset request is submitted; confirmation message is shown

steps.content: Immediately navigate back and submit the same email again (or tap Submit multiple times if the button remains active)
steps.expected: Second request is submitted

steps.content: Repeat the submission a third time in quick succession
steps.expected: Third request is submitted or rate limiting is triggered

steps.content: Observe the system response for subsequent requests
steps.expected: Either a rate-limiting message is displayed (e.g., 'Too many requests. Please wait before trying again.') or the system silently accepts but does not spam the user's inbox

testCases.expectedResult: The system handles rapid successive reset requests gracefully. Rate limiting is applied if configured. The user's inbox is not flooded with multiple reset emails. The app does not crash.

testCases.title: The email field should display a validation error when an email without the @ symbol is entered on the registration form
preconditions: App is installed and launched on a mobile device
User is on the Registration screen

steps.content: Navigate to the Registration screen
steps.expected: Registration screen is displayed

steps.content: Enter 'plaintext' (no @ symbol) in the email field
steps.expected: Text is entered in the field

steps.content: Tap on the password field or tap the Submit button to trigger validation
steps.expected: Validation is triggered on the email field

steps.content: Observe the email field state
steps.expected: An inline validation error is displayed indicating the email format is invalid (e.g., 'Please enter a valid email address')

testCases.expectedResult: The system rejects the malformed email and displays an inline format validation error. No API call is made.

testCases.title: The email field should display a validation error when an email with only the domain part is entered (e.g., @domain.com)
preconditions: App is installed and launched on a mobile device
User is on the Registration screen

steps.content: Navigate to the Registration screen
steps.expected: Registration screen is displayed

steps.content: Enter '@domain.com' in the email field
steps.expected: Text is entered in the field

steps.content: Tap on the password field or tap the Submit button to trigger validation
steps.expected: Validation is triggered

steps.content: Observe the email field state
steps.expected: An inline validation error is displayed indicating the email format is invalid

testCases.expectedResult: The system rejects the malformed email '@domain.com' and displays an inline format validation error.

testCases.title: The login form should display a validation error when the email field is submitted empty
preconditions: App is installed and launched on a mobile device
User is on the Login screen

steps.content: Navigate to the Login screen
steps.expected: Login screen is displayed with empty fields

steps.content: Leave the email field empty
steps.expected: Email field remains empty

steps.content: Enter any value in the password field
steps.expected: Password is entered and masked

steps.content: Tap the Login / Sign In button
steps.expected: Form submission is attempted

steps.content: Observe the form state
steps.expected: An inline validation error is displayed on the email field. No API call is made.

testCases.expectedResult: The system prevents submission and displays an inline error on the empty email field. The user remains on the Login screen.

testCases.title: The login form should display a validation error when the password field is submitted empty
preconditions: App is installed and launched on a mobile device
User is on the Login screen

steps.content: Navigate to the Login screen
steps.expected: Login screen is displayed with empty fields

steps.content: Enter a valid email address in the email field
steps.expected: Email is entered in the field

steps.content: Leave the password field empty
steps.expected: Password field remains empty

steps.content: Tap the Login / Sign In button
steps.expected: Form submission is attempted

steps.content: Observe the form state
steps.expected: An inline validation error is displayed on the password field. No API call is made.

testCases.expectedResult: The system prevents submission and displays an inline error on the empty password field. The user remains on the Login screen.

testCases.title: The user session should persist when the app is backgrounded and reopened after a successful login
preconditions: App is installed and launched on a mobile device
User has successfully logged in with valid credentials
User is on the post-login screen (e.g., dashboard)

steps.content: Press the device home button to background the app
steps.expected: App is moved to the background

steps.content: Wait for approximately 30 seconds
steps.expected: App remains in the background

steps.content: Reopen the app from the recent apps list or app icon
steps.expected: App is brought to the foreground

steps.content: Observe the screen displayed
steps.expected: The user is still logged in and the post-login screen (e.g., dashboard) is displayed without requiring re-authentication

testCases.expectedResult: The user session persists across app backgrounding and reopening. The user is not redirected to the Login screen unexpectedly.

testCases.title: The user should be able to log in successfully when a valid Google account is selected via SSO
preconditions: App is installed and launched on a mobile device
User is on the Login screen
A dedicated Google test account is available and authorized for the staging OAuth client
Device has active network connectivity

steps.content: Navigate to the Login screen
steps.expected: Login screen is displayed with a 'Sign in with Google' option

steps.content: Tap the 'Sign in with Google' button
steps.expected: The native Google account picker is displayed

steps.content: Select the designated Google test account from the account picker
steps.expected: OAuth authorization flow is initiated

steps.content: Wait for the OAuth handshake to complete
steps.expected: Authorization is granted and the app receives a valid OAuth token

steps.content: Wait for the system to process the token and create/retrieve the user session
steps.expected: User is authenticated and navigated to the post-login screen within 3 seconds

testCases.expectedResult: The user is successfully authenticated via Google SSO. A valid session is created. The user is redirected to the post-login screen. The OAuth token is not exposed in logs or analytics events.

testCases.title: The SSO flow should display an appropriate error when the user cancels the Google account picker
preconditions: App is installed and launched on a mobile device
User is on the Login screen
Device has active network connectivity

steps.content: Navigate to the Login screen
steps.expected: Login screen is displayed

steps.content: Tap the 'Sign in with Google' button
steps.expected: The native Google account picker is displayed

steps.content: Tap the Cancel or Back button on the Google account picker
steps.expected: The account picker is dismissed

steps.content: Observe the app state
steps.expected: The user is returned to the Login screen. An appropriate message or no error is shown (cancellation is not treated as a failure). The app does not crash.

testCases.expectedResult: The app handles the user-cancelled SSO flow gracefully. The user is returned to the Login screen without an error state. No session is created.
